title: Flowise SSRF protection bypass: tools call raw node-fetch/axios instead of the secure HTTP wrapper
description: Flowise's SSRF mitigation (introduced via httpSecurity.ts / secureFetch / secureAxiosRequest with HTTP_DENY_LIST, IP-resolution validation, IP pinning and loopback blocking) is opt-in rather than enforced. Multiple tool/loader nodes import raw HTTP clients (node-fetch, axios) directly and fetch user-controlled URLs without routing through the secure wrapper. In packages/components/nodes/tools/OpenAPIToolkit/OpenAPIToolkit.ts the OpenAPI 'link' input destructures the user-supplied openApiLink and calls `await fetch(openApiLink)` using raw node-fetch (imported at the top of the file), so none of the deny-list/IP-pinning checks run. An attacker who can drive such a tool (e.g. via chat prompting the agent to fetch a URL) can force the server to request internal addresses such as the cloud metadata endpoint http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/, exfiltrating internal resources and credentials.
